key_points: Er:YAG laser radiation delivered through a flexible waveguide achieves antibacterial effect in root canal walls and surrounding tissues.
Therapeutic doses of Er:YAG laser radiation enable one-step disinfection of the root canal, including elimination of anaerobic microorganisms.
The use of a flexible waveguide allows delivery of Er:YAG radiation into curved root canals, expanding clinical applicability.
Calcium hydroxide is mentioned in the context of endodontic disinfection protocols.
The approach targets dentin and periapical tissues for disinfection during endodontic treatment.

outcomes.measured: Antibacterial effect in root canal and surrounding tissues
outcomes.result: Er:YAG laser via flexible waveguide achieved disinfection including anaerobic microorganisms
outcomes.clinical_significance: One-step disinfection of root canal system reported

notes: Full text not available; extraction based on abstract only. Study type is unclear from abstract — may be clinical case series or in vitro/ex vivo study. Specific laser parameters not reported in abstract.
source_citation: Dostálová, T., Jelínková, H., Houšová, D., Šulc, J., Němeć, M., Dušková, J., Miyagi, M., & Krátký, M. (2002). Endodontic Treatment with Application of Er:YAG Laser Waveguide Radiation Disinfection. Journal of Clinical Laser Medicine & Surgery. https://doi.org/10.1089/104454702760090218
target_tissue: dentin, root canal walls, periapical tissue

drugs_mentioned.name: calcium hydroxide
drugs_mentioned.dental_relevance: Mentioned in context of endodontic disinfection protocols alongside Er:YAG laser treatment
